message:
Add shared library compilation with --binary and --force flags

Major changes:
- Default: Compile to .so files (fibonacci_arm64.so)
- --binary flag: Compile to standalone binary
- --force/-f flag: Force recompile (ignore cache)
- Architecture detection (x86_64, arm64, etc.)

Implementation:
- src/main.zig: New CompileOptions struct, flag parsing, architecture detection
- src/compiler.zig: New compileZigSharedLib() function using zig build-lib -dynamic
- Proper handling of .so files (can't execute directly)

Usage:
  pyx myapp.py                     # Fast: builds myapp_arm64.so
  pyx myapp.py --force             # Force recompile
  pyx myapp.py --binary            # Build & run standalone binary
  pyx build --binary myapp.py      # Build binary for deployment
  pyx build myapp.py -f            # Force rebuild .so

Results:
- ✅ Default builds .so (1.1M)
- ✅ --binary builds standalone executable (1.1M)
- ✅ Both support caching and --force
- ✅ Architecture-specific naming (myapp_x86_64.so)
- ⏳ .so runner coming next (currently suggests --binary)

This follows industry standard (NumPy/Cython model) for development speed
while keeping standalone binary option for production deployment.
